100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On May 31, 1926, the British General Strike came to an end after nearly nine days of labor unrest, with workers returning to their jobs amid negotiations for better wages and conditions. In the United States, a notable heatwave gripped several states, causing discomfort and raising concerns about health and safety. Meanwhile, in Germany, political tensions escalated as the Weimar Republic faced challenges from both the left and right, hinting at the instability that would shape the country's future.
Updated: Sunday, May 31, 2026 at 08:03:16 AM CDT
